findings.title: Escalation threshold inconsistency: '2+' vs '>2' for dependencies
findings.description: Spec line 150 scenario has depends_on with 2 items and expects escalation with reason 'depends on >2 packages', but the requirement says 'depends on 2+ other packages' (inclusive). Either the scenario should use 3 deps or the log message should say '>=2'.
findings.suggestion: Change the scenario to use 3 dependencies, or change the requirement threshold and log message to '>=2'.

findings.title: Contracts README says 'no HTTP API changes' but Phase 3 extends /work/submit and /work/claim
findings.description: Contracts README states the feature does not introduce or modify HTTP API endpoints, but tasks 3.3.2 adds agent_requirements to /work/submit and /work/claim. These are parameter extensions that constitute contract changes.
findings.suggestion: Update contracts README to acknowledge the /work/submit and /work/claim parameter extensions as additive API changes.

findings.title: Ambiguous resolution layer: where does resolve_model() get called?
findings.description: Design D4 says CliVendorAdapter.dispatch() should use archetype_model, but it's unclear whether skills call resolve_model() before dispatching or if the Agent tool itself resolves archetypes. The resolution layer (skill vs. dispatcher vs. agent tool) needs clarification.
findings.suggestion: Add explicit callsite documentation in design.md: skills call resolve_model() with package metadata dict, then pass resolved model to dispatch().

findings.title: D7 merge-train coexistence claim has no cross-feature test scenario
findings.description: Design claims archetype and merge-train routing are orthogonal, but no spec scenario tests both dimensions simultaneously (archetype + stack_position on the same task).
findings.suggestion: Add a scenario testing claim() with both archetype requirement and merge-train constraint.

findings.title: wp-queue-routing locks API endpoints it doesn't modify
findings.description: wp-queue-routing locks 'api:POST /work/submit' and 'api:POST /work/claim' but only modifies work_queue.py. The API endpoints are modified in wp-api-mcp-integration (task 3.3.2).
findings.suggestion: Move API endpoint locks to wp-api-mcp-integration. Keep only 'db:schema:work_queue' in wp-queue-routing.

findings.title: Missing scenario for archetypes.yaml file not found at runtime
findings.description: D5 specifies graceful degradation if archetypes.yaml is deleted, but no spec scenario tests this. The system should log a warning and fall back to ambient model.
findings.suggestion: Add scenario: WHEN archetypes.yaml does not exist, THEN load_archetypes_config() returns empty dict with warning log.

findings.title: Backward compatibility scenario already covered but phrasing differs
findings.description: Spec has 'Task without archetype requirement claimable by any agent' which covers legacy tasks, but the phrasing 'backward compatible' in parentheses could be more explicit about pre-archetype tasks.
findings.suggestion: Minor: rename scenario to 'Legacy task without archetype requirement claimable by any agent' for clarity.
